entries.definition:
b-q-r cow; to split up, to gash, to plough, to enlarge; to travel
around; corruption. Of this root, three forms occur nine times in
the Qurʾan: بَقَر baqar three times; بَقَرَة baqaratun four times and
بَقَرَات baqarāt twice.

بَقَر baqar [coll. n., sing. fem. بَقَرَة baqaratun, pl. بَقَرَات baqarāt]
cows (2:67) وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تَذْبَحُوا بَقَرَةً remember
when Moses said to his people, ‘God commands you to sacrifice a
cow’; * البقرة name of Sura 2, Medinan sura, so named because of
a reference in verses 2:67–71 to ‘the cow’ which the Israelites
were ordered to slaughter.
